data.title: A whale transferred 2,540 ETH to Binance, incurring a loss of approximately $2.4 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, an address transferred 2,540 ETH to Binance after holding them for approximately three months, worth about $5.56 million. This address had previously withdrawn 2,550 ETH from Binance, worth about $8 million, representing a loss of approximately $2.4 million based on current prices.

data.title: The European Central Bank supports consolidating crypto regulation under the ESMA, aiming to unify the EU's regulatory framework.
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has expressed its support for a proposal to centralize the regulatory authority for crypto assets within the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA). Headquartered in Paris, ESMA could become the unified regulatory body for crypto assets across the EU. Currently, EU crypto regulation is largely fragmented among member states, and while based on the MiCA framework, differences exist in enforcement standards and licensing procedures. This proposal aims to reduce regulatory arbitrage, improve cross-border coordination efficiency, and strengthen investor protection. According to the plan, ESMA may be responsible for regulating stablecoin issuers, supervising trading platforms, and approving related white papers, while the ECB will focus on macro-financial risk assessment. Industry insiders believe this move could reduce cross-border compliance costs, but the specific scope of authority and transition arrangements still need further clarification. (CrowdfundInsider)

data.title: Exodus CEO: Institutional investors are accelerating their entry into the crypto market, which may be entering an "institution-dominated cycle."
data.description: Exodus CEO JP Richardson stated that institutions are accelerating their entry into the crypto market this year, while retail participation has declined, suggesting the market may be entering a new institutional-driven phase. He pointed out that stablecoin market capitalization is currently at an all-time high, and traditional financial institutions are continuing to invest, including launching Bitcoin ETFs and establishing crypto-related business units. MN Fund founder Michaël van de Poppe also stated that under inflationary and cost-of-living pressures, retail funds are limited, and this cycle may be characterized by institutional dominance and a longer duration. Furthermore, CryptoQuant data shows that inflows into small accounts holding less than 1 BTC on Binance have fallen to a near nine-year low, indicating declining retail participation. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Data: The top 3 CEXs by XAU contract trading volume in the past 24 hours are Binance, Bitget, and OKX.
data.description: According to CoinGlass data, XAU is currently trading at $4717.28, down 0.56% in the last 24 hours. Possibly influenced by geopolitical tensions such as the breakdown of the first round of US-Iran negotiations, market volatility has increased. The total trading volume of XAU contracts across all exchanges reached $1.339 billion in the last 24 hours, a 504.45% increase compared to the previous period. Among centralized exchanges, liquidity is mainly concentrated on Binance, Bitget, and OKX, with 24-hour trading volumes of $644 million, $207 million, and $171 million respectively, ranking in the top three.

data.title: South Korea Warns of Crypto Market Abuse via APIs
data.description: South Korea's Financial Supervisory Service has issued a warning to consumers regarding increasing abuse in the cryptocurrency market linked to APIs. According to NS3.AI, these application programming interfaces are responsible for approximately 30% of the nation's virtual asset trading volume. The regulator highlighted that automated trading programs are being utilized to artificially inflate transactions and manipulate market conditions. In response, a consumer advisory was released, providing examples of such misconduct to raise awareness among investors.

data.title: Genius has added a refund feature to its airdrop program, supporting refund requests within 48 hours of receiving a TGE (Take-It-Your-Times) refund.
data.description: Genius announced on its X platform that it has added a "refund" function to its airdrop program based on community feedback. Users can choose to burn all their GENIUS airdrop shares within 48 hours after the TGE event in exchange for a refund of the net transaction fees charged by the platform. The refund is calculated based on net transaction fees and does not include previously distributed trading rebates or referral rewards; it only covers Genius's own fees and does not include third-party protocol fees. The platform stated that whether to hold the tokens or apply for a refund is entirely up to the user.

data.title: Oil Prices Surge Above $105 Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions
data.description: Oil prices have climbed above $105 following the breakdown of U.S.-Iran discussions in Islamabad, which has reignited concerns of potential conflict in global energy markets. According to NS3.AI, the Kobeissi Letter reported that Google search interest for 'price of oil' has reached an unprecedented level, surpassing the peaks of 2022 and 2008 by 300%.

data.title: Western Australia Considers Strategic Diesel Stockpile Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Western Australia is evaluating the establishment of a strategic diesel stockpile to address fuel shortages impacting critical sectors like farming and mining. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the shortages have arisen due to the ongoing conflict involving Iran. The state plans to finance the fuel reserves, aiming to secure essential supplies for its industries. This initiative reflects a proactive approach to mitigate the disruptions caused by geopolitical tensions affecting global fuel availability.

data.title: Whale Investor Faces Losses on Iran Regime Prediction
data.description:
A significant investor, identified by the address 0xa7d1...b824, has incurred a loss of $345,000 after investing $527,000 in a prediction that the Iranian regime would collapse by June 30. According to Odaily, this investor previously placed $176,000 on a prediction that the U.S. military would invade Iran by March 31 and $148,000 on the regime's collapse by the same date, both of which did not materialize.

The market's criteria for a 'yes' outcome are stringent, requiring the dissolution, paralysis, or replacement of Iran's current governing system, including the Supreme Leader's office, the Guardian Council, and the Revolutionary Guards led by clergy, by June 30, 2026. This must be confirmed by a consensus among recognized authoritative media, indicating the regime has lost control over the majority of the population. The rules specify that regular political elections, internal reforms, or power transitions do not meet the criteria. Even an internal coup retaining the core structure of the Islamic Republic would result in a 'no' settlement. Additionally, the loss of some territory or challenges from anti-government forces would not constitute a 'collapse' unless it prevents the regime from governing the majority of the population.

data.title: Polkadot Bridge Exploit Mints 1 Billion DOT Amid Geopolitical Concerns
data.description:
A recent midday cryptocurrency digest reported a significant security breach involving the Polkadot network. According to NS3.AI, the exploit resulted in the unauthorized minting of 1 billion DOT tokens, raising concerns about the network's security protocols.

In addition to the Polkadot incident, the digest highlighted geopolitical tensions, specifically the potential implications of a U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Such an action could escalate risks in the region, affecting global markets and investor sentiment.

data.title: AI Convergence 2026 Set to Explore AI and Web3 Opportunities in Hong Kong
data.description: AI Convergence 2026 is scheduled for April 20, 2026, at the AWS HK Office in Causeway Bay, Hong Kong. According to NS3.AI, the event is jointly hosted by Bixin and TRON DAO, and will concentrate on the implementation and commercial opportunities of AI and Web3 technologies.

data.title: Web3 Payments and Stablecoins to Take Center Stage at RWA & Payments 2026 Event
data.description: RWA & Payments 2026 is set to take place on April 20, 2026, at the AWS Hong Kong Office in Causeway Bay, Hong Kong. According to NS3.AI, the event is being jointly hosted by Mask Network, BenPay, and StableStock. It will focus on discussions around Web3 payments, stablecoins, real-world assets (RWA), and on-chain financial infrastructure.

data.title: Hong Kong Police Report Romance Scam Involving Cryptocurrency
data.description:
A recent report from Hong Kong police's 'Cyber Defender' platform reveals a romance scam resulting in a loss exceeding HKD 2 million. According to BlockBeats, the scam involved a fraudster who initiated contact with a woman in her fifties on Instagram, quickly establishing a romantic relationship. The scammer posed as a 'cryptocurrency investment expert,' promising guaranteed returns.

The victim was first asked to transfer HKD 40,000 as an 'account opening fee.' Subsequently, she was persuaded to exchange cash for USDT at a physical exchange store in seven separate transactions, transferring the funds to the scammer's electronic wallet. Once the funds were secured, the scammer disappeared and cut off all communication.

Police advise the public to exercise caution when forming online relationships. If someone claims to be an investment expert and requests money transfers for any reason, individuals should pause and reconsider before proceeding.

data.title: Hong Kong and China Sign Memorandum to Boost AI and Blockchain Collaboration
data.description: Hong Kong's Secretary for Innovation, Technology and Industry, Sun Dong, and Wang Jingtao, Deputy Director of China's Cyberspace Administration, have signed a memorandum of cooperation. According to Foresight News, the agreement focuses on advancing key areas such as artificial intelligence, cross-border data, and blockchain. The initiative aims to implement China's 15th Five-Year Plan, support Hong Kong's development as an international innovation and technology hub, and promote a new economy driven by technological innovation. The collaboration seeks to enhance Hong Kong's integration into national development strategies. Both parties will strengthen cooperation to advance new productivity, deepen cybersecurity collaboration, and enhance international exchanges and promotion.

data.title: U.S. to Impose Blockade on Iranian Ports Amid Tensions
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump announced on his social media platform that the United States will enforce a blockade on ships entering and exiting Iranian ports at 10 a.m. Eastern Time on April 13. According to BlockBeats, this decision follows unsuccessful negotiations between the United States and Iran.

On April 12, President Trump expressed indifference towards Iran's willingness to return to the negotiating table. He reiterated the plan to impose the blockade and voiced disappointment over NATO's lack of support for the U.S. in the conflict involving Iran.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Strategists: U.S. Inflation Impact from Middle East Ceasefire Could Be Temporary
data.description: On April 13, Deutsche Bank strategists released a report indicating that the U.S. Treasury market will need to assess the impact of the Middle East conflict ceasefire on U.S. inflation. According to Jin10, if the ceasefire persists, the inflation surge is likely to be viewed as a temporary phenomenon. The strategists expect the Federal Reserve to resume interest rate cuts in September. They also highlighted the need to monitor the war's potential impact on Treasury issuance, as Congress might allocate additional funds for the military. Deutsche Bank strategists anticipate that any increase in issuance will remain moderate and primarily involve long-term financing, helping to avoid pressure on long-term yields.

data.title: Hyperbridge Gateway Contract Attacked, Hacker Gains $237,000
data.description: PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). Hyperbridge's gateway contract has been compromised in a recent attack, according to CertiK Alert. The attacker manipulated the administrator privileges of the Polkadot token contract on Ethereum by forging messages. This breach allowed the hacker to mint and sell one billion tokens, resulting in a profit of approximately $237,000.

data.title: Nine newly created wallets withdrew 87.76 million Binance Life tokens from Binance, worth $14.9 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, in the past 24 hours, nine new wallets withdrew 87.76 million Binance Tokens from Binance, worth $14.9 million.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | UBP Increases Gold Holdings, Forecasts Year-End Price to Reach $6,000
data.description: Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) has increased its gold holdings, projecting that the price of gold will reach $6,000 by the end of the year. According to Jin10, the bank's decision to bolster its gold reserves reflects a strategic move in response to anticipated market conditions. UBP's forecast suggests a significant rise in gold prices, driven by factors such as global economic uncertainties and inflationary pressures. The bank's outlook aligns with broader market sentiment, where investors are increasingly turning to precious metals as a hedge against volatility. This move by UBP underscores the growing importance of gold in investment portfolios amid fluctuating financial markets.
